rulings.Case Title: DORAN MCGEE ET AL VS. BROWN FAMILY TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Entryy Of Default Pursuant To Ccp 473; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Cathy Elliott Jones In Support Thereof; Exhibits; Proposed Demurrer And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT BROWN FAMILY TRUST, SANDRA MCGEE Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Entryy Of Default Pursuant To Ccp 473 On November 1, 2021 this Court issued a minute order directing the moving party to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers. A stack of loose unbound, unstapled papers was delivered to the Court in response to the minute order. Defendants' motion is GRANTED based on CCP 473(b) mandatory relief provisions (se Cathy Elliott Jones Declaration). Defendants shall file and serve answer no later than December 3, 2021. Defendants and their counsel shall comply with all CCP, CRC and SFLR provisions in the future. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HARRY BOWER VS. 3RE5 LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2021 line 4. Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Injunction is GRANTED. Preliminary Injunction shall issue on the same terms as the Temporary Restraining Order. If any further issues with utilities/locks shall arise, the landlord shall within 24 hours either fix the issue or appear ex-parte to address reasonable time and accommodations to address the issue. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
